def: s.f. Perception, comprehension, knowledge.
s.m. Coming and going; intercourse, &c. ( = ānā-jānā, āwā-jāʼā, &c.); ingress and egress; being born and dying; transmigration.
more: n. f. H. ānā to come, S. गम् to go. 1. Coming and going; ingress and egress ( ānā jānā ). Nit ke āvā-gavan te nipat an-ādar hoë, Yā te nit nit par-graha bhūli jāū mat koë . Doha. From constant visiting esteem doth fall away. This maxin bear in mind, nor daily visits pay. 2. Transmigration of souls ( jīvan-maran ).

def: part. adj. Detested; blamed, reproached; wicked, vile; — s.m. Blame, reproach, obloquy; satire.
s.m. Defect, blemish, fault, demerit, worthlessness, evil, vice; harm, mischief; contempt, disregard ( = S. avagan); a corrupt or vicious person ( = augunī)
more: E; n. m. S. अव off, away, गुण quality. 1. Want of goodness or virtue; demerit; badness. Champā, to meṅ tīn gun raṅg, rūp aru bās; Augun tujh meṅ kaun sā, bhaṅvar na baiṭhe pās . Dohā. Fair form, rich color, and sweet scent, Champā , three merits thine, What then thy fault, the beetle woos thee not— all too supine. 2. Vice; wickedness; evil; evil deeds. 1. Mere augan chit na dharo re . Bhajan. Regard not, O Lord, the evil that is in me! 2. Gun sīkh-ke, augan sīkho . Prov. He learns good and does evil. 3. Defect; fault; flaw; failing; blemish. Mujh meṅ kyā augan hai? 4. Immorality; looseness of morals; impropriety; sin. Augan nandī ko chhailā sikhābat hai, Khet pe le jāt saṅg merī nand ko, Chhailā toṛ-ke bhauṅṭā chabābat hai . Rus. Song. This rake corrupts my husband's sister, to The field he takes her with him thereunto, An Indian corn he gives to her to chew. 5. Disease; disorder; malady…
subs: (-ko), To impute a fault, vice, &c. (to), to slander, defame.

def: s.f. Disregard, disrespect, contempt, despite
subs: adj. Contemptuous, degrading, derogatory; — s.m. A contemptuous person, &c.

def: s.m. Sending for, calling, summoning, inviting; call, summons; invitation.
s. f. Report.
more: 1. s. f. (from آنا To come) Approach, advance. 2. A saddlecloth adorned with fringes, &c.

def: n. f. 1. Report; rumour; gossip; hearsay; false rumour or report. 2. News of one's arrival, coming or advance ( avātī ). avāī uṛānā , v. a. To spread a false report.
s.f. Approach, advance; proximity; arrival; report or news of (one's) approach or arrival ( = āvātī); report, rumour, hearsay ( = afwāh); — a saddle-cloth adorned with fringes; — a kind of pickaxe.
s.f. Approach, advance; proximity; arrival; report or news of (one's) approach or arrival ( = āvātī); report, rumour, hearsay ( = afwāh); — a saddle-cloth adorned with fringes; — a kind of pickaxe.
s.m. pl. (of ʻaib, q.v.), Blemishes, defects; faults, vices.
s. m. f. plur. (of اوّل) Commencements, beginning.
s.m. (f. ?), Beginnings; beginning, first part, early portion or period; the first ten days of a lunar month (the remaining decades are termed awāsit and awāḵẖir, q.q.v.)
subs: awāʼil-ʻumr, s.f. Early youth, tender age, child hood; — s.m. A youth
adv. In the beginning at first.

def: adj. (f. awaiʼī), Future, yet to come
s. f. Coming and going, intercourse, dancing after.
s.m. Insurance (syn. bīmā).
part. Ascertained, concluded, certain.
s.m. Leisure, opportunity ( = ausar); interval, space, room, way, scope.
more: s. m. 1. Leisure, opportunity. 2. Interval, space.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit अवकाश (avakāśa).

def: adj. (f. inī), Unfruitful, barren.
s.m. plur. (of āḵẖir), Ends, latter parts, extremes; the last ten days of a lunar month.
more: s. plur. (of آخر) Ends, latter parts.

def: s.f. pl. (of ʻāqibat, q.v.), Ends, &c.; consequences; — posterity.
s.m. Enclosed space formed by a cluster of peasant's houses; a string fastened round the wheel of a distaff.
more: E. n. f. 1. An enclosed space formed by a cluster of rāyat's houses. 2. A string fastened round the wheel of a distaff ( jatnī , W).

def: adj. Of inauspicious or evil omen; — unfortunate; — s.m. Bad omen.
adj. Hanging down, depending, hanging on or upon; — s.m. Asylum, protection, dependence, support.
s.m. Depending (upon), supporting oneself (upon); dependence, reliance; support, prop.
— see اولمب avalamb.
adj. Depending or hanging (on), dependent (on); delaying, dilatory; — s.m. Dependent; refugee; procrastinator.
part. adj. Suspended (from), depending (on), trusting (to), supported (by); protected, cherished; alighting, descending.
s.f. Row, range, continuous line, series; lineage, dynasty
more: n. f. 1. Misfortune; disaster; misery. 2. Mortal danger. See alī .

def: s.m. Looking at, beholding; sight, view, inspection.
part. Seen, looked at, inspected; — s.m. A saint of the Jain sect.
v.t. To look at, see, inspect, view.
s.m. Leaping suddenly (upon), breaking forth, rushing on or away
s.m. Debt, loan; — colour (see wām).
more: s. Debt, loan: colour: see وام wām.

def: adj. Undermost, inferior, lowest, base, vile, low; growing less, decreasing; — s.m. A lunar day exactly coinciding with a solar one.
wety: From Sanskrit ॐ (oṁ).

def: s.m. pl. (of A. عامة ʻāmmat, fr. ʻām, q.v.), The commonalty, the common people, the common sort, the vulgar, the populace
more: n. m. pl. of عامه 1. The common people; the mass; populace; vulgar. 2. The scum or dregs of society. avām-un-nās ; H. sab log , The people; the community; the population; the public. avām kī ittilā ke līye mushtahar hotā hai . Is hereby promulgated for general information. avām ke fāëde ke līye . For the public good or welfare. avām ke līye . For the public; common.
s. plur. (of عامّة) The vulgar, populace, common people. عوام الناس aﻌwāmmu-n-nās, The vulgar, common people.
subs: The common people, the populace at large, the public.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عوام ('awām), from Arabic عَوَامّ (ʕawāmm), the plural of عَامَّة (ʕāmma). Compare Punjabi ਅਵਾਮ (avām).

def: s.m. Kiln = āwā, q.v. A Sanskrit prefix denoting 'from,' 'down from,' 'off,' 'away.' (It is the reverse of आ ā, and is sometimes used to denote negation. deprivation, disgrace, disjunction, &c.)
s.m. Coming, approach, &c. ( = awāʼī, q.v.)
more: n. m. W. The iron ring round the nave.
(see آنا) s. m. Coming, approach. آون آون āwan āwan, s. f. Tidings of arrival.
subs: s.m. Tidings or news of arrival.

def: s.m. The field into which rice has been transplanted (cf. āman).
s.m. Iron band round the navs of a wheel.
n. m. ānā to come. Tidings or news of arrival.
s.f. plur. (of ān, 'time'), Times, seasons.
s.m. pl. (of عون ʻaun), Aids, helps; assistants, aiders.
(in Braj) v. n. To come.
s.m. Vessel, pan.
more: (comp. of آب and وند) s. m. A vessel, a pan.

def: see s.v. āwanā or āʼonā.
s.f. The earth, world; — avanī-pa, s.m. Lord of the earth (an epithet of a king or ruler).
more: pron. (dakh.) They, them, to them, he, she, it.

def: s.f. Name of a city, the modern Oujain.
s.f. Name of a city, the modern Oujain.
more: s. f. Name of a city, the modern Oujein.

def: s.f. Delay; — adv. Late, &c. = aver, q.v. s.f. Delay, &c.; — adv. Late, &c. (see aber)
subs: awerī-sawerī, adv. Late and early, at all hours, unseasonably; late or early, late in the evening or early in the morning
v.n. To delay, loiter, dally, to procrastinate.

def: s.m. Account-book, &c. = āmār and avārija, q.q.v.; also = āwāra, q.v. s.m. Heap, pile. (The word would appear to be peculiar to the Panjāb.)
s.m. Injustice, tyranny ( = S. अपकार).
s.m. Injustice, tyranny ( = S. अपकार).

r: āwar, āwardan, ā, war, bar, zor-āwar
ety: rt. of āwardan; ā+war = Zend bar = S. भर्], Bringing, bearing; possessing, endowed with (used as last member of compounds, e.g. zor-āwar
def: adj. Having strength, strong, powerful).
more: part. act. (from آوردن) in compos. Bringing, bearing, bringer, bearer, producing.

def: adj. Low, inferior, hinder; — s.m. Low person; a śūdra.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀯𑀭 (avara), from Sanskrit अपर (ápara, “other, different”). Cognate with Punjabi ہوْر (hor).

def: adj. Separated from one's family ( = judā); without house and home; wandering, roving; astray; abandoned, lost; dissolute; — s.m. Wanderer; vagabond; profligate
more: adj. 1. Roving; wandering about; separated from one's family. 2. Abandoned; lost; destitute of name or character. āvārah phir'nā , Lurking. G. G. To stroll about; wander up and down. Kharāb khastā, nāj sastā, āvarah phirtā hai . Prov. saying. Ruined and lost he is tossed about like cheap grain. āvārah kar'nā , v. a. To make a vagabond of; to spoil; corrupt (kharāb k.). āvārah honā , v. n. The verbal form of اَواره q. v.

adj. Vagabond, wanderer, dissolute. آواره هونا āwāra honā, To wander, to be distressed, to be oppressed, to be miserable. آواره کرنا āwāra karnā, To make a wanderer, to expel, to harass. آواره گردي āwāra-gard-ī, s. f. Vagrancy, wandering about. (Plur. آوارگان).
subs: To wander or rove about; to wander astray; to go loafing about
(-ko), To drive out of house and home; to make a vagabond of; to ruin
To be separated from one's family, &c. (see āwāra).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آوَارَه (āwāra).

def: s.f. Wandering; vagrancy; profligacy.
more: s. f. Wandering, the state of a wanderer, vagrancy, profligacy.

def: s.m. Covering; a covering, shield, protection; obstruction, lock.
adj. Not natural, artificial, false; affected; assumed (opp. to āmad, 'that which comes natural').
more: adj. lit . brought. Opp. of āmad what comes naturally. Artificial; affected; unnatural; false; far-fetched.

def: s.f. Period of life, &c. = āyurdī, q.v.
more: s. f. The allotted period of any oneʼs life, a life time, an age.

ety: perf. part. of āwardan; see āwar
def: s.m. That which is brought or carried over
more: n. lit . That which is brought over. One who is favored; a protogè.
part. pass. (of آوردن) Brought. (Sans. आ and ऊढ Borne, from r. वहु Bear).
subs: one who is taken into favour
s.m. Registrar or writer of accounts as delivered.

def: adj. (in Gram.), Not classified.
s.m. Account-book; day-book; note-book, diary, journal; book of receipts and disbursements.
more: n. m. 1. A day book ( roz-namchah ); diary; journal. 2. Abstract account of collections and remittances; a book of receipts and disbursements ( jama-kharch bahī ).

def: s.m. Account-book; day-book; note-book, diary, journal; book of receipts and disbursements.
more: s. m. A journal, a diary.

def: s.m. pl. of ʻāriẓ, or ʻāriẓa, qq.v.
s.m. = āwārija, q.v. s.m. Account-book; day-book; note-book, diary, journal; book of receipts and disbursements.
s.m. Stoppage, obstruction, hindrance, resistance, restraint, confinement; — the seraglio of a palace, the queen's apartment
s.m. Absence of impediment, non-opposition, assent, concurrence; absence of contention, tranquillity, quiet.
s.m. Turning; turn; curl; whirlpool, eddy.
part. Hindered, restrained, stopped, obstructed, kept in order, controlled; besieged, imprisoned, shut up, confined, secluded.
s.m. House, dwelling, habitation.
wety: From Sanskrit आवास (āvāsa).

def: adv. Certainly, &c. = avaśya, q.v. adj. Necessary, &c. = avaśyak; — adv. Certainly, assuredly, of course, to be sure, it is true, inevitably, indispensably
adj. Unsubmissive to another's will, not under control, unsubdued, unrestrained, disobedient; — not having one's own free will, subject to another, without choice, helpless, powerless.
s.m. End, conclusion, completion; boundary, limit; death, destruction.
s.m. Occasion, fit moment, time or season (of), favourable opportunity; vacation, leisure
subs: To find occasion, get an opportunity; to have or find leisure
To watch for an opportunity, to observe the time or season (of); to bide (one's) time.

def: s.m. Residue, &c. = avaśesh, q.v.
s.m. Residue, remainder, surplus; end, close, termination, appendix, supplement (syn. tatimma).
s.f. Unripe corn (chiefly barley) cut from time to time and brought home to be eaten.
more: Bhoj. n. f. Unripe corn cut from time to time and brought home to be eaten without being threshed.

def: = avaśya, q.v. adj. Necessary, &c. = avaśyak; — adv. Certainly, assuredly, of course, to be sure, it is true, inevitably, indispensably
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀑𑀲𑀸 (osā), from Sanskrit अवश्या (avaśyā). Cognate with Bengali ওস (ōs).

def: part. = ava-śishṭ.
part. Left, remaining.
s.m. pl. (of ausat̤), Middles, means, averages; the period of a lunar month between the tenth and twentieth days.
s.f. State, condition, circumstances ( = ḥāl); age; life; story, narrative (of a life).
more: n. f. S. अव well, स्था to stand. 1. State; condition; age. 2. Period; duration; life ( ārbal ). Yeh itnī hī austhā le-kar ayā thā . 3. Story; narrative; history. Rājā ne sārī avasthā kahī .
s. f. State, condition (of humanity varying with the progress of time, as youth, age, &c.).

def: adj. Unreal, unsubstantial, vain, trifling; unessential, not belonging to the true nature of a thing; — s.m. A worthless thing, nothing, &c.
s.f. Unreality, insubstantiality; non-essentialness.
adj. Necessary, &c. = avaśyak; — adv. Certainly, assuredly, of course, to be sure, it is true, inevitably, indispensably
s.m. Anything necessary, necessity, expediency; — adj. Necessary, indispensable, inevitable, actual.
s.m. Anything necessary, necessity, expediency; — adj. Necessary, indispensable, inevitable, actual.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit आवश्यक (āvaśyaka).

def: s.f. Necessity, need, indispensableness, inevitability.
s.m. Crest; ear-ring; ornament, gem; wealth; halter, rein.

s.m. Descent (especially of a deity from heaven), appearance of any deity upon earth; incarnation (more particularly of Vishṇu in ten principal forms, viz. 1˚ Matsya, 'the fish'; 2˚ Kaććhapa, 'the tortoise'; 3˚ Varāha, 'the boar'; 4˚ Nr̤i-singha, 'the man-lion'; 5˚ Vāmana, 'the dwarf'; 6˚ Paraśu-rāma; 7˚ Rāma; 8˚ Balarāma, or Kr̤ishna; 9˚ Buddhā; 10˚ Kalkī); — any new and unexpected appearance or phenomenon; any pious or distinguished person (in the language of respect or flattery); a wicked, depraved or quarrelsome person (ironically); — crossing ( = utār); translation; a tīrth or sacred place of Hindū pilgrimage
more: n. m. S. अवतार from अवतृ to descend, Pr. oāra . 1. An incarnation of the deity. ☞ The Hindūs reckon ten incarnations of the god Vishnu:—1st, Machh a fish; 2nd, Kachh a tortoise; 3rd, Bārāh a boar; 4th, Nar-Singh a man-lion; 5th, Baunā or Bāman a dwarf; 6th; Paras Rām ; 7th, Rām; 8th, Krishn; 9th, Budh; 10th, Kalkī or Niskalank , which is yet to appear on the culmination of the kal-yug the iron age or age of sin. 2. A pious or distinguished person, in the language of flattery, is also called an autār . 3. ( Ironic ). A bad, wicked, or quarrelsome person; a wrangling jade ( uṭār ). 4. A mighty, powerful person; a powerful king or authority. Tape rāj aṅgrez kā, jāne jag, sansār, Yā kal-jug ke bīch meṅ, yeh bhī baṛe autār . Dohā. Bright shines the English rule, as all the world doth know, An incarnation great this iron age doth show. autār dhārnā, yā lenā , v. n. To become incarnate; to come…

s. m. Descent, especially of a deity from heaven; incarnation. (The Hindūs reckon the following ten avatārs of the God Vish̤ṇu: 1. मत्स्य matsya, A fish: 2. कच्छप kachchhapa, A tortoise: 3. वराह varāha, A hog: 4. नृसिंह nr̤i-singha, A man-lion: 5. वामन vāmana, A dwarf: 6. परशुराम parasurāma: 7. राम rāma: 8. बलराम balarāma: 9. बुद्ध buddha: 10. कल्की kalkī ).
subs: autār lenā, v.n. To become incarnate, to appear in the world (a deity).
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit अवतार (avatāra).

def: v.n. To descend (especially as an incarnation of the deity = autār lenā); to cross, &c. = utarnā, q.v.
more: v. n. To descend (as an incarnation of the Deity, &c.) on earth.

def: = avatarnā, q.v.
s.f. = awātī, q.v. s.f. Approach, coming; advance; coming of an army; approach of a friend; season in which merchandise is expected (cf. awāʼī).

n. f. 1. Coming; approach; advance. Lag rahī hai avātī basant kī, āī na pī kī pātī ho! Song. Basant . The Spring is almost come, no letter from my husband still! 2. News of one's coming; tidings; aviso. āë din , adv. Every day; day after day (nit). Ise to āë din nayā kapṛā chāhiye . + Wom.
s.f. Approach, coming; advance; coming of an army; approach of a friend; season in which merchandise is expected (cf. awāʼī).
more: s. f. Approach, advance; the coming of an army, the approach of a friend; the season in which merchandise is expected.

def: part. Descended; crossed, passed over; translated.
s.f. = ʻaf-ʻaf, q.v. s.f. An imitative sound (as of a dog barking), bow-wow.
more: n. f. An imitative sound, as a bark, bow-vow. av av k ; H. bhauṅ bhauṅ k., bhauṅknā , v. a. To bark.
s. (dakh.) An imitative sound (as, of barking): see عف عف aﻌf aﻌf.

def: n. f. pl. of اول Beginnings. Youth ( chhoṭī umar ). — —, n. m. A youth.
s.m. A limb, member; part, portion; member or component part of a logical argument or syllogism.

ety: Pehl. āfāj, Zend ā+vać, S. वाच्
def: s.f. Sound, noise; voice, tone; whisper; echo; shout, call, cry; report, fame; sentence
more: n. f. 1. Sound; echo; voice; whisper; tone. 2. Call; shout; cry; street cry; mendicant's cry (sadā) . Pukāre hai koī āvāz kas-ke, "Karāṛe, bhurbhure, nībū ke ras ke ." Saīyad. Hear this one in the street cry out, "my gram who'll buy," "Crackling and crisp and lemon-flavored," come and try. 3. Creak; clank; twang; jingle; jar; rustle; tick (of a clock): ringing (of a bell); blast (of a trumpet); report; crash. 4. Note; intonation; tune. 5. Din; roar; uproar; peal; hue and cry; whoop. 6. Reputation; fame; good name. Terī āvāz Makke, Madīne meṅ! Prov. May your voice be heard in Mecca and Medina! (A benediction on one who brings good tidings). āvāz uṭhā nā, baṛhā'nā, yā ūṅchī karnā, v. a . To raise the voice; speak up; to speak in a loud voice; to strain the voice. āvāz ān'ā , v. n. To become audible; to be heard. āvāz bharrā'nā , v. n. To become hoarse and husky. Kul bāje baj-kar ṭūṭ gaë, ā…

s. f. Voice, sound, report, fame, echo, a whisper, sentence. آوازاٿهانا āwāz uṭhānā, To raise the voice, to spread a report. آوازپڙنا āwāz paṛnā, 1. To be reported. 2. The voice to be suppressed (from hoarseness or otherwise).
s. m. (dakh.) Voice, &c. آوازپرکان رکهنا āwāz par kān rakhnā, To listen, to hear or hearken.
subs: To raise the voice, to speak out; to strain the voice; to spread a report
āwāz ūṅćī baṛhānā, To raise the voice ( = āwāz uṭhānā)
āwāz paṛnā, or paṛ-jānā, To become hoarse from cold, singing, &c.; to lose the voice; — āwāz-baiṭhā, adj. Hoarse
or rakhnā, or lagānā, To lend an attentive ear, to hear, listen
(kān-meṅ), To happen to hear, to catch a sound; to overhear; to be heard, to be reported
(-kī), To answer to, or to obey, a call
(-ko), To call out (to), shout (to), to hail; — v.n. To give forth a sound; to answer a call
āwāz-kā ṭappā, s.m. Range or reach of voice; pitch of the voice; hearing-distance, ear-shot
(-kī), To bring forth a sound or report (from), cause to sound; to fire off (a gun, &c.)
To call or cry out (one's wares); to beg; to raise the voice (in singing)
To sing in harmony or concert (with)
To give out a sound; to bring forth or produce a sound; to speak.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آواز (āvāz), from Middle Persian ʾwʾz (āwāz). Cognate with Sanskrit वाच् (vā́c) and Latin vōx. First attested in c. 1665 as Middle Hindi آواز (āvaz).

def: s.m. Noise; loud talk = āwāz; report; rumour, fame
more: s. m. Report, fame, rumour, conversation, talking aloud.
subs: āwāza kasnā, āwāza mārnā (-par), To talk (of), make insinuations (about); to speak in innuendoes; to jeer (at)

def: n. m. Noise. See āvāz (4). Fame; reputation. (Rare).

n. m. H. bolī ṭholī , n. f. Taunts; jeers; inuendoes; insulting expressions. āvāzah kas'nā,—pheknā,—mār'nā , v. n. To speak in inuendoes; speak at; taunt or jeer at; laugh at. Kisī kī bahū beṭī pe āvāzah mat pheko .

n. f. H. āo come, S. भक्ति attachment. Courteous welcome (commonly feigned). Jaisī terī āo-bhagat, waisī merī āshirbād . Prov. As your reception, so my adieus. āo-bhagat kar'nā , v. a. To receive a person with due ceremony and respect; to welcome. Sūrat se faqīr thā birogī, Kī āo-bhagat samajh-ke jogī . Nasīm.
s.m. The ocean = abdhi, q.v. s.m. The ocean. — abdhi-kaph, s.m. Cuttle-fish bone.

ety: perf. part. of āweḵẖtan, Zend ā+rt. vij; S. ā+vij or vić
def: part. adj. Suspended, hanging, pendulous, pendent; attached, connected.
s.f. Delay, &c.; — adv. Late, &c. (see aber)
more: adv. Late; too late. See aber. Diye jag-jotī, terī māyal pirove motī, Terī dhannī bichhāve khāṭ, Terī bahnal dekhe bāṭ, Tū sidausī āīyo, averī jāīyo, Jasaudā mātā se Rām, Rām kahiyo . Wom. Song. Diyā-bāṛhāvān . (Sung by Hin. women when putting out the light). O lamp that to the world gives light, Your Ma a string of pearls threads bright, Your wife has spread for you your bed, Your sister waits your footstep's tread, Early come back and late hence flee, Jasodā mother greet for me. aver saver, averī saverī adv. Late or early; late in the evening or early in the morning. aver karnā, der karnā , v. a. To delay; dawdle; loiter; take one's time; put off to the last ( āj kal k . 1.). aver honā , v. n. v.n. To be free, unfettered, independent, &c.; to be set at liberty, to gain or obtain (one's) freedom, &c.; to be discharged or released (from); to escape; to break prison; to throw off the yoke; to be free from care; to be unconcerned or indifferent; to hold cheap or in contempt.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آزَاد (āzād).

def: part. adj. Free; noble, &c. = āzād, q.v. part. adj. Free, unfettered, unrestrained, uncontrolled; liberated, discharged, set free, ransomed, emancipated; free born; free from care, at ease, light-hearted; — s.m. A freeman; freedman; a faqīr or Mohammadan devotee who shaves his beard and eye-lashes and vows chastity, but considers himself exempt from all the ceremonial observances of religion; a free-thinker
subs: v.t. To set free or at liberty; to free, deliver, release, liberate; to manumit, emancipate; to absolve; to dismiss; to extricate
s.m. The āzād's or faqīr's staff; (Met.) An impudent or shameless person

v.n. To be free, unfettered, independent, &c.; to be set at liberty, to gain or obtain (one's) freedom, &c.; to be discharged or released (from); to escape; to break prison; to throw off the yoke; to be free from care; to be unconcerned or indifferent; to hold cheap or in contempt.

def: adj. Free, noble. (Plur. آزادگان).
s.m. Those who are free from worldly cares; religious men, &c. (See āzād.)
s.f. Freedom; release, deliverance, liberation, discharge; manumission, emancipation; freedom of actioṇ, liberty, independence; conduct, life or practice of an āzād or devotee.
more: s. f. Freedom, independence, release, manumission, conduct, life or practice of an آزاد āzād or hermit.

def: s.f. Freedom; release, deliverance, liberation, discharge; manumission, emancipation; freedom of actioṇ, liberty, independence; conduct, life or practice of an āzād or devotee.
more: n. f. 1. Release; enlargement; deliverance; emancipation; liberation; discharge. 2. Freedom of action; power; liberty; full play; latitude; freedom; independence.
s. f. Freedom, independence, release, manumission, conduct, life or practice of an آزاد āzād or hermit.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آزادی (āzādī). Equivalent to آزاد (āzād) + ـی (-ī).

def: adj. compar. & superl. (of ضعيف ẓaʻif), More, or most, powerless or helpless; very weak; very helpless.
more: adj. compar. (of ضعیف) Very weak or helpless, most impotent.

def: adj. pl. (of ضعف ẓěʻf), Double.
s.f. Eternity without beginning (opp. to abad, q.v.); existence from eternity; beginning, source, origin.
more: n. f. Ped. for H. ād . Beginning; source; origin. roz-i-azal , n. m. The day of one's birth on which his destiny is registered.
wety: From Arabic أَزَل (ʔazal).

ety: for A. عضلة aẓalat, v.n. fr. عضل 'to prevent,' &c.
def: s.m. A muscle, tendon, sinew (syn. ʻaṣab).
s.m. pl. (of next, q.v.), Tendons, muscles, &c.
adj. Eternal without beginning; from all eternity.
more: adj. Eternal without beginning, from all eternity.

def: s.f. Existence from eternity.
more: s. f. The being eternal without beginning, eternal existence.

def: adj. compar. & super. (of ذليل ẕalīl), More, or most, vile; baser; basest; more abject; most abject.
more: adj. compar. (of ذلیل) More or most vile, base or abject.

def: adj. compar. & superl. (of عظيم ʻaz̤īm), Greater; greatest; very great.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أَعْظَم (ʔaʕẓam).

ety: for A. عظمة, v.n. fr. عظم 'to be or become great'
def: s.f. Greatness, magnitude; aggrandizement; grandeur, magnificence; pomp, pride, haughtiness, self-magnification ( = ʻuz̤m).
more: n. f. عظم was great. Greatness; magnificence ( baṛāī , 5, 6).
(from عظم) s. f. Greatness, magnificence, magnitude, aggrandizement.
ex: The spiritual grandeur of the Pakistani Army
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَظَمَت (azamat), from Arabic عَظَمَة (ʕaẓama). First attested in c. 1635 as Middle Hindi عظمت ('z̤mt /⁠'azmat⁠/).

def: s.f. The notification or announcement of prayer and of the time thereof; the call to prayer (usually chanted from the turret of a mosque)
more: n. f. Rus. bāṅg , اِذن listened. The summons to prayers proclaimed from the house top, or the minaret or tower of a mosque. — de'nā , v. a. To summon or call to prayers.
s. f. The summons to prayers (generally proclaimed from the minarets or towers of a mosque).

def: s.m. Sickness, disorder, disease, infirmity; trouble, affliction; injury, outrage
more: n. m. 1. Sickness ( rog ); disease; disorder; in. firmity. 2. Injury; trouble; affliction; outrage. āzār'denā , v. a. 1. To trouble; injure; vex; torment; cause, produce, give, or inflict pain; molest ( dukh denā ). 2. To persecute; oppress; hurt, or wound one's feelings ( dil dukhānā ). 3. To cause grief, uneasiness, or trouble, etc.

1. s. m. Sickness, disorder, disease, trouble, affliction, injury. 2. Part. act. in compos. (of آزردن) Troubling, afflicting, as in مردم آزار mardum-āzār. آزارده āzār-dih, adj. Vexatious, troublesome. آزار دهي āzār-dih-ī, s. f. Vexatiousness, troublesomeness.
subs: (-ko), To vex, trouble, torment, inflict pain, to pain; to molest; to persecute; to hurt, injure.

ety: rt. of āzārdan; ā + rt. Zend, zar; S. har
def: part. act. Vexing, troubling, afflicting, &c. (used in comp., e.g. dil-āzār, 'afflicting or paining the heart').
s. The sixth of the Chaldean or Syro-Macedonian months; March.
s.m. The sixth of the Greek (or Syrian) months, corresponding to March, O.S.
prop. n. The name of Abraham's father (com. but incor. written آذر āẕar, q.v.).
s.m. Fire; — the ninth month of the Persian Calendar, December; winter; — (prob. for A. āzar) the name of Abraham's father.
more: s. m. 1. Fire. 2. The name of Abrahamʼs father (Terah). 3. The ninth solar month; winter.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آذَر (āḏar), from Middle Persian 𐭭𐭥𐭥𐭠 (NWRA /⁠ādur⁠/)

r: āzarda, āzurda, āzardan, ā, zar, har
ety: perf. part. of āzardan; ā+rt. Zend zar = S. har]
def: part. adj. Afflicted (by, -se), sad, dispirited, sorrowful; vexed (with, -se), displeased, dissatisfied; weary (of, -se)
more: adj. Afflicted, sad, dispirited, sorrowful, vexed, displeased, weary. آزرده خاطر āzurda-ḵẖāt̤ir or آزرده دل āzurda-dil, Vexed, troubled in mind, offended, disgusted, dejected, sad. آزرده خاطري āzurda-ḵẖāt̤ir-ī, s. f. Sadness, &c. آزرده کرنا āzurda karnā, To afflict, vex, displease, offend.
subs: āzurda-dil, adj. Grieved in heart, troubled in mind; sad, dejected; offended, vexed, displeased; dissatisfied; disgusted
v.t. To vex, annoy, displease, offend; to afflict; to grieve.

def: s.f. Lispleasure, vexation, annoyance; grief, sorrow; dissatisfaction; ill-humour; chagrin.
more: n. f. اَزردن to vex. 1. Displeasure; vexation; dissatisfaction; grief; annoyance. 2. Mortification; chagrin; ill-humour.
s. f. Displeasure, vexation, affliction, sorrow.

def: adj. Of or relating to the month Āẕār; vernal.
more: adj. Of the month āẕār, vernal.

def: adj. Of or relating to Āẕar, q.v.
s.m.f. Sick; troubled, afflicted; a sick person, a patient.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آزاری.

def: s.m. Camomile flower, ox-eye.
s.f.(?) Modesty; mildness.
s.m. Name of a fallen angel; a devil, an evil spirit.
more: n. m. A fallen angel; Satan (ابليس).
n. prop. Name of a devil or fallen angel.

def: adv. By rote; by heart; from memory; without a book; memoriter. az jā'nib , adv. On the part of; in behalf; on the side of; for; from; by. az had' , adj. Indefinite; boundless; unlimited; immeasureable; infinite; unutterable; unapproachable; unspeakable; beyond expression; unrestricted; surprising. az had' , adv. In a high degree; highly; deeply; strongly; mightily. az khud , adv. Of one's own accord; himself; of one's own will or act; voluntarily; suo motu. az khud raf'tah , n. Out of one's mind; beside oneself; non compos . az rū'e , adv. From the face or appearance of; by reason of; according to; by; in consequence of; by virtue of; under the circumstances. az rū'e iske , By these presents. az rū'e haq , Ped. Equitably; justly. az rū'e qānūn' , Legally; according to the law. az sar tā pā , Ped. for H. sir se pāoṅ tak . 1. From head to foot; from top to toe; from head to heel. 2. In toto ; altogether; wholly. 3. Throughout; through; en masse . 4. Perfectly; completely; totally; entirely; thoroughly; cap-a-pie ; every inch; wholesale. Phr. From end to end; from beginning to end; from first to last. az sar-i-nau , Ped. for H. naë sire se . Anew; afresh; over again. az taraf , adv. On behalf of; on the part of; on the side of; from; in the interest of; in favor of. az gaibī , adj. Hidden; mysterious. az kī az , Ped. for H. joṅ kī toṅ . Exactly; precisely; neither more nor less; just the thing; the very thing; to a hair. Jo kuchh bāt thī, az kī az kah sunāī .
more: adv. By heart, by rote, by memory.

def: n. m. A large serpent; a dragon; a python ( ajgar ).
adj. Worthy; becoming, fit, proper, suitable.
more: adj. Fit, becoming, worthy. ¶ for اژدر azhdar, q. v.

def: = اژدر azhdar, q.v. s.m. A large serpent, python, boa constrictor ( = ajgar); a dragon.
s.f. Worthiness, fitness.
adj. (compar. & super. of ذفر ẕafir), Of strong, or very strong, odour (whether sweet or unpleasant); very strong-scented; most fragrant.
s.m. (pl. of ذهن ẕihn), Geniuses; intellects; capacities.
more: s. plur. (of ذهن ẕihn ) Geniuses, intellects, capacities.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَذْهَان (azhān), from Arabic أَذْهَان (ʔaḏhān).

def: adj. compar. & superl. (of ظاهر z̤āhir), Clearer; clearest; more evident; very evident
more: adj. compar. (of ظاهر) Very clear, more evident. اظہر من الشمس az̤ʼharu mina-sh-shamsi, Clearer than the sun.

def: s. (dakh.) Basilisk, dragon ( Harris ): see اژدها azhdahā.

ety: azh = Pehl. aj, Zend azhi, S. ahi, 'dragon,' 'serpent'; dahā = Pehl. dahāk, Zend dahāka, 'destructive'; rt. dah = S. दास्
def: s.m. A large serpent, python, boa constrictor ( = ajgar); a dragon.
more: s. m. A large snake, a dragon. (Plur. اژدهات).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَژْدَهَا (aždahā). First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi اژدہا (aždha).

ety: azh = Pehl. aj, Zend azhi, S. ahi, 'dragon,' 'serpent'; dahā = Pehl. dahāk, Zend dahāka, 'destructive'; rt. dah = S. दास्
def: s.m. A large serpent, python, boa constrictor ( = ajgar); a dragon.

ety: azh = Pehl. aj, Zend azhi, S. ahi, 'dragon,' 'serpent'; dahā = Pehl. dahāk, Zend dahāka, 'destructive'; rt. dah = S. दास्
def: s.m. A large serpent, python, boa constrictor ( = ajgar); a dragon.
s.m. Mixed metal, bell-metal.

part. adj. Determining, being resolved (on); intending; applying the mind to an undertaking; bound (for a place).
more: part. act. (of عزم) Applying the mind to an undertaking, intending, determining, being resolved on, bound (somewhere).

def: adj. Great, high in dignity; of great account or estimation; — large, vast, enormous, excessive, severe, grievous; difficult, burdensome, &c.
more: adj. See baṛā , 1, 5, 8, 13, 14. Excessive; extreme. azīm-ul-shān . Magnificent; splendid. harj-i-azīm . Much inconvenience.
(from عظم) adj. Great, high in dignity, large. عظيم الشان aﻌz̤īmu-sh-shān, adj. Of high station or dignity, magnificent.
subs: adj. Of high station or dignity; magnificent, splendid; very large or lofty (buildings, or trees, &c.).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic عَظِيم (ʕaẓīm).

ety: for A. عزيمة, inf. n. of عزم 'to determine,' &c.
def: s.f. Determination, &c. ( = ʻazm, q.v.); — an obligatory statute or ordinance of God ( = ʻazmat); — a charm, spell, incantation; power of compelling spirits to attend on one.
s.m. Chanting the Qorʼān; hearing.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِذْن (izn), from Arabic إِذْن (ʔiḏn).

def: part. adj. & s.m. Excusing, making excuses; — an excuser, an apologist; — mark of a wound, a scar.
more: part. act. (of عذر) 1. Making excuses. 2. s. m. An apologist. 3. The scar of a wound.

def: s.f. Wanton injury; molestation, annoyance, oppression; trouble, suffering, distress
more: s. f. Wanton injury, oppression, distress, vexation, trouble, suffering, torment. اذیت دینا aẕīyat denā, To injure, harass, annoy, torment.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَذِیَت (aziyat), from Arabic أَذِيَّة (ʔaḏiyya).

ety: v.n. fr. عزّ; see ʻizz, and ʻizzat
def: adj. & s.m. Dear, worthy, precious, highly esteemed, greatly valued, honoured, respected, beloved; — a great man; a worthy or pious personage, a saint; one beloved, a dear friend; a relation, relative; — a great potentate, a title of the king of Egypt; — the plant Centaury
more: adj. عز esteemed. 1. Respected; honored. 2. Precious. 3. Dear (پيارا). 1. Achchhī chīz, sab ko azīz hai . Prov. A good thing is dear to all. 2. Jān se azīz kuchh nahīṅ . Prov. Nothing so dear as life. azīz , n. m. 1. One beloved; a dear friend. 2. A relation; relative. 3. A title of the king of Egypt. azīz-ul-qadar , My dear Sir. A complementary form of address to subordinate officers in official or private correspondence. azīz-dārī , n. f. Relationship ( rishtahdarī ). azīz rakhnā, yā jānnā , v. n. 1. To esteem or value highly; to revere. 2. To love.

(from عزّ) 1. adj. Dear, darling, worthy, respected, beloved, honoured, esteemed, excellent, precious, friend. 2. s. m. (1.) A title of the king of Egypt. (2.) Centaury. (Pers. plur. عزيزان Friends). عزيزجاننا يا رکهـنا aﻌzīz-jānnā or -rakhnā, To esteem or value highly, to love, respect, revere.
subs: or ʻazīz rakhnā (-ko), To hold dear or precious, to esteem or value highly, to love; to revere
s.m. A near and dear friend or relative
Mv honoured or worthy friend, my dear sir (a form of address to subordinate officers in official or private correspondence).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عزیز ('azīz), from Arabic عَزِيز (ʕazīz).

ety: ʻazīz, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Greatness; sanctity; dearness, friendship, respect.
s.m. Repetitions of the names and praises of God, recitals, commemorations, rehearsals of prayer.
more: s. m. plur. (of ذکر ẕikr ) Praises, praises of God, recitals, commemorations.

def: s.m. People of discernment, penetration or understanding; the acute; the ingenious.
more: s. plur. (of ذکي ẕakī ) People of discernment, the acute, the ingenious.

ety: inf. n. of عزل 'to put away or aside,' 'to remove,' &c.
def: s.m. Removing, dismissing; removal (from office)
subs: To remove and to appoint; to make promotions and reductions.

def: v. a. lit. to appoint and dismiss. To make promotions and reductions.
s.f. Ribs; sides (of a figure); districts, divisions (of land); convexities; arches.
more: s. plur. (of ضلع ẓilﻌ ) Ribs, sides; districts, divisions (of land); convexities, arches.

def: adj. compar. & superl. (of ظالم z̤ālim), More, most, or very unjust, tyrannical or oppressive, &c.
more: adj. compar. (of ظالم) More or most unjust, tyrannical, &c.

ety: ʻazl, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Retirement; secession; abdication.
s.m. Determination, resolution, decision, intention, fixed purpose, bent, aim; undertaking
more: n. m. See (اراده 2, 3, 5). 1. Aim; bent. 2. Undertaking. azm bil-jazm ; H. A. pakkā irādah . Settled purpose; firm resolve; determination; tenacity of purpose. azm bil-jazm k. , v. a. See pakkā irādah k. azm k. , v. a. To determine; resolve ( irādah k , 2).
s. m. Design, intention, purpose, undertaking, resolution, determination.
subs: v.n. To make a resolve, to resolve, determine, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَزْم (azm), borrowed from Arabic عَزْم (ʕazm), from عَزَمَ (ʕazama).

def: s.m. Bone.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عظم ('azm), from Arabic عَظْم (ʕaẓm).

def: (from āzmūdan, 'to test,' see āzmūda), part. act. Trying, trial (used in comp.).
s.f. Trial, test, proof, essay, examination; experiment
subs: v.n. To be put to proof or to the test; to be experienced.

def: s.f. Trial, test, proof, essay, examination; experiment
subs: v.n. To be put to proof or to the test; to be experienced.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آزمایش (āzmāyiš).

def: n. f. P.
more: (from آزمودن) s. f. Trial, proof, examination, essay, experiment.

def: s.m. pl. (of زمن zaman), Times, seasons.
more: s. plur. of زمان or زمانه q. v.

ety: fr. āzmā, rt. of P. āzmūdan
def: v.t. To try, prove, test, essay, examine; to try conclusions with.
more: v. a. 1. To examine; try; essay; subject to trial ( kasnā ). 2. To test; prove; make trial of; to put, bring, or submit to the test or proof; verify. 3. (Slang.) To test (a man's virility). Tum ne āzmāyā hogā, jo ḍhīlā (weak) batātī ho . 4. To join issue; to bandy, or try conclusions with; to fight with. Jis meṅ dam ho, sāmne āve, aur ham ko azmāve .
v. a. To try, to prove, to examine; to touch gold, &c. on the touchstone.
wety: From Classical Persian آزما (āzmā) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: adj. Covetous, avaricious (see āz).

ety: for A. عزمة, inf. n. of عزم; see ʻazm
def: s.f. Determination, resolution, &c. ( = ʻazm); — a thing that is obligatory (of those that God has made obligatory); the divine decree.
more: s. f. 1. See عزم aﻌzm. 2. The divine decree.

def: s.m. pl. (of زمان zamān), Times, seasons ( = azmān).
more: s. plur. of زمان or زمانه q. v.

ety: perf. part. of āzmūdān; = ā+zi+rt. Zend mā = S. मा
def: part. adj. Tried, tested, proved, examined; experienced
more: part. pass. (of آزمودن) Tried, proved, examined. آزموده کار āzmūda-kār, Experienced in business.
subs: adj. & s.m. Experienced (in business, or in deeds of high emprise), practised, skilled; clever; — an experienced person; a veteran; a proficient
(-kā), To make trial of, to try, &c. = āzmānā.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آزموده (āzmūda).

def: s.f. = آزمائش āzmāʼish, q.v. s.f. Trial, test, proof, essay, examination; experiment
subs: v.n. To be put to proof or to the test; to be experienced.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آزمودگی (āzmūdagī, “experience”), from the verb آزمودن (āzmūdan, “test, to experience”).
